summary: Build Final UX/Design Spec with Visual References

message.content: os arquivos do harness desse projeto estão muito grandes, precisamos fazer comprimi-los de modo que nenhum detalhe por menor que seja possa comprometer a qualidade do harness e dos resultados das proximas interacoes.

content.thinking:
O usuário quer comprimir os arquivos do harness do projeto porque estão muito grandes. Ele quer manter todos os detalhes importantes para não comprometer a qualidade do harness e dos resultados das próximas interações.

Preciso primeiro entender quais são os arquivos do harness e qual o tamanho atual de cada um. Os arquivos principais do harness são:
1. CLAUDE.md - Instruções do projeto
2. claude-progress.txt - Histórico de sessões
3. features.json - Features com status
4. docs/*.md - Documentação
5. agent_docs/*.md - Documentação técnica

Vou começar analisando o tamanho de cada arquivo e depois propor uma estratégia de compressão.

content.text: Vou analisar os arquivos do harness para entender o tamanho atual e propor uma estratégia de compressão.

input.command: find . -name "*.md" -o -name "*.json" -o -name "*.txt" | grep -E "(CLAUDE|claude-progress|features|docs/|agent_docs/)" | head -30 | xargs wc -l 2>/dev/null | sort -n
du -h CLAUDE.md claude-progress.txt features.json docs/*.md agent_docs/*.md 2>/dev/null | sort -h
input.description: Check file sizes of harness files

content.thinking:
Os arquivos do harness são bastante grandes. Vou listar os maiores:

1. claude-progress.txt - 92K (o maior!)
2. docs/RESEARCH.md - 48K
3. docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md - 40K
4. docs/MMS_STYLE_GUIDE.md - 36K
5. CLAUDE.md - 24K
6. docs/MMS_PRESENTAION.md - 24K
7. docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md - 24K
8. docs/UX_RESEARCH.md - 20K
9. docs/PRODUCT_PLAN.md - 20K
10. docs/PARALLEL_EXECUTION.md - 20K
11. docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md - 16K
12. docs/mms-components-research.md - 16K
13. docs/DOMAIN.md - 16K
14. features.json - 16K (290 linhas)

A estratégia de compressão deve:
1. Manter todos os detalhes essenciais
2. Remover redundância
3. Consolidar informações dispersas
4. Arquivar histórico antigo do claude-progress.txt

Vou ler os arquivos principais para entender o conteúdo e propor uma estratégia.

content.text: Vou analisar o conteúdo dos maiores arquivos para entender o que pode ser comprimido.

content.content: 1→# Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log 2→ 3→## Session: 2025-12-19 (DESIGN_SPEC.md Creation) - IN PROGRESS 🔄 4→ 5→### Task: Create Implementation Design Specification Document 6→ 7→**Objective:** 8→Create `docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md` - an internal technical specification document that will guide the implementation of the demo with Astro.js + deck.gl. Focus on the Planning View with Sandbox Mode for what-if scenarios. 9→ 10→**Key Decisions:** 11→- Source of truth: ONLY `docs/RFQ.md` (ignore existing code/docs) 12→- Stack: Astro.js + deck.gl + mocks 13→- Language: English 14→- Primary feature: Planning View with Sandbox Mode (what-if scenarios) 15→- Theme: Dark mode enterprise 16→ 17→**RFQ Pain Points to Solve:** 18→1. No dedicated mileage management tools 19→2. IBM Maximo only does asset management 20→3. Manual Excel management 21→4. Folder-driven data storage 22→5. Limited maintenance-operations integration 23→6. Non-compliance risk (underrun/overrun) 24→7. No predictive simulations 25→8. Insufficient resource visibility 26→9. Manual handling delays 27→ 28→**Progress:** 29→- ✅ Research: deck.gl, what-if scenario patterns, dashboard design 30→- ✅ Collected visual references (Kepler.gl, Linear, Figma templates) 31→- ✅ Created `docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md` with complete specifications 32→- ✅ Detailed Sandbox Mode (what-if scenarios) as primary feature 33→- ✅ Added moodboard with `` tags for visual references 34→ 35→**Document Created: `docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md`** 36→ 37→Contents: 38→1. Visual Mood & Design Philosophy (Linear as reference) 39→2. Moodboard with `` tags (Kepler.gl, Flowmap, SandDance, Figma templates, Railfleet) 40→3. Problem → Solution Matrix (all 9 RFQ pain points) 41→4. Design System (colors, typography, spacing, radius) 42→5. Screen Specifications (Application Shell, Navbar, Sidebar) 43→6. Planning View - Semantic Zoom (MACRO/MESO/MICRO) 44→7. **Sandbox Mode** (PRIMARY FEATURE) - complete specification 45→ - Mode Toggle UI 46→ - Visual Differentiation (violet accent) 47→ - Sandbox Panel with delta metrics 48→ - Sandbox Interactions (drag, resize, create, delete) 49→ - Compare View (baseline vs scenario) 50→ - Saved Scenarios management 51→8. Components Specification (TaskBlock, KPICard, AlertBanner, Minimap) 52→9. Data Structures (Train, Task, Scenario entities) 53→10. Interactions & Gestures 54→11. Performance Targets 55→12. Implementation Stack (Astro.js, deck.gl, XState, Tailwind) 56→13. File Structure (proposed) 57→14. RFQ Requirements Mapping 58→ 59→**Status: COMPLETE ✅** 60→ 61→--- 62→ 63→## Session: 2025-12-18 (Semantic Zoom POC) - COMPLETE ✅ 64→ 65→### Task: Complete Semantic Zoom transitions POC with crossfade 66→ 67→**Objective:** 68→Test and verify the Semantic Zoom POC that implements smooth crossfade transitions between 4 LOD levels using Hermite smoothstep interpolation. 69→ 70→**Completed:** 71→ 72→1. **Semantic Zoom POC Story:** 73→ - `apps/canvas/src/stories/01-POCs/Rendering/SemanticZoom.stories.tsx` 74→ - 4 LOD levels: Strategic (<0.3x), Tactical (0.3-1.0x), Operational (1.0-3.0x), Execution (>3.0x) 75→ - Crossfade transitions with smoothstep Hermite interpolation 76→ - Layer opacities panel showing real-time opacity percentages 77→ - Interactive zoom slider with threshold markers 78→ 79→2. **LOD Level Visualizations:** 80→ - **Strategic (0.15x):** Blue heatmap with percentage cells (50%, 65%, 78%, etc.) 81→ - **Tactical (0.60x):** Train IDs list (T-001 to T-016) with quarterly bars label 82→ - **Operational (2.00x):** DNA Gantt view with orange styling 83→ - **Execution (4.00x):** Detailed task cards with train ID, task type (PM1/PM2/PM3/Overhaul), status badges (completed/in-progress/scheduled) 84→ 85→3. **Crossfade Transitions Verified:** 86→ - Strategic→Tactical (at 0.3): Tested at 0.34x - strategic: 30%, tactical: 70% 87→ - Tactical→Operational (at 1.0): Tested at 0.94x - tactical: 78%, operational: 22% 88→ - Operational→Execution (at 3.0): Tested at 2.94x - operational: 78%, execution: 22% 89→ - All transitions use smooth Hermite interpolation (no sudden jumps) 90→ 91→4. **Story Variants Created:** 92→ - Default: Interactive slider for testing all transitions 93→ - At Strategic Level: Pre-set to 0.15x 94→ - At Tactical Level: Pre-set to 0.60x 95→ - At Operational Level: Pre-set to 2.00x 96→ - At Execution Level: Pre-set to 4.00x 97→ 98→**Testing Results:** 99→- All 4 LOD levels render correctly with distinct visuals 100→- Crossfade transitions are smooth and use correct opacity blending 101→- Interactive slider allows seamless navigation between LOD levels 102→- No console errors 103→ 104→**Run POC:** 105→```bash 106→cd apps/canvas && bun run storybook 107→# Navigate to 01-POCs/Rendering/Semantic Zoom 108→``` 109→ 110→**POCs Completed This Session:** 111→- ✅ POC: deck.gl heatmap for Strategic view (previous) 112→- ✅ POC: Gesture recognizer (pan, pinch, momentum) (previous) 113→- ✅ POC: Semantic zoom transitions (crossfade) ← THIS SESSION 114→ 115→**Next Tasks:** 116→- Components: GanttBar, KPICard, TodayLine, TimelineHeader 117→- Layer: Strategic (12-year heatmap) 118→- Layer: Tactical (stacked bars) 119→- Layer: Operational (DNA Gantt) 120→- Layer: Execution (detailed Gantt with resources) 121→ 122→--- 123→ 124→## Session: 2025-12-18 (XState Viewport Machine POC) - COMPLETE ✅ 125→ 126→### Task: Create XState viewport state machine for semantic zoom 127→ 128→**User Request:** 129→Build POCs for the MMS Fractal Canvas demo, starting with XState v5 for state management. 130→User decisions: Use XState (not @mms/toolkit), BlueprintJS 6, ignore dark mode. 131→ 132→**Completed:** 133→ 134→1. **Project Structure Setup:** 135→ - Created folder structure: `01-POCs/`, `02-Layers/`, `03-Demo/` 136→ - Removed default Storybook example pages 137→ - Installed XState 5.x and @xstate/react 6.x 138→ 139→2. **Mock Data Configuration:** 140→ - `apps/canvas/src/stories/data/mock-config.ts` - Fleet config, LOD thresholds, colors 141→ - `apps/canvas/src/stories/data/mock-generator.ts` - Generates 60K+ tasks over 12 years 142→ - 128 trains (49 Kinki Sharyo + 79 Alstom), 2024-2036 contract period 143→ 144→3. **XState Viewport Machine:** 145→ - `apps/canvas/src/stories/machines/viewport.machine.ts` 146→ - States: `idle`, `panning`, `pinching`, `momentum`, `animating` 147→ - Events: ZOOM, ZOOM_TO, PAN, PAN_START/MOVE/END, DOUBLE_TAP, RESET, etc. 148→ - LOD levels: strategic (<0.3x), tactical (0.3-1.0x), operational (1.0-3.0x), execution (>3.0x) 149→ - Smooth animations with easeOutExpo easing 150→ - Momentum/inertial panning support 151→ 152→4. **XState Viewport POC Story:** 153→ - `apps/canvas/src/stories/01-POCs/State/XStateViewport.stories.tsx` 154→ - Interactive canvas with pan/zoom via mouse/touch 155→ - Real-time State Inspector showing all context values 156→ - Quick Action buttons: Strategic (0.2x), Tactical (0.5x), Operational (1.5x), Execution (4x) 157→ - LOD indicator with color-coded thresholds 158→ 159→5. **Bugs Fixed:** 160→ - TypeScript type imports (use `import type` for ESM compatibility) 161→ - ANIMATION.zoom → ANIMATION.duration.zoom (nested object access) 162→ - Animation interpolation using saved start values (not current values) 163→ 164→**Testing Results:** 165→- All LOD transitions working correctly 166→- Smooth animations between zoom levels 167→- State machine transitions properly (idle → animating → idle) 168→- Context values update correctly in real-time 169→ 170→**Run POC:** 171→```bash 172→cd apps/canvas && bun run storybook 173→# Navigate to 01-POCs/State/XStateViewport 174→``` 175→ 176→--- 177→ 178→## Session: 2025-12-18 (Storybook Time Scrubber POCs) - COMPLETE ✅ 179→ 180→### Task: Set up Storybook and create POCs for Time Scrubber components 181→ 182→**User Request (Portuguese):** 183→"Configure o Storybook no projeto e adicione POCs para todas as melhorias, sugestões, libs, opções listadas no docs/mms-components-research.md" 184→ 185→**Completed:** 186→ 187→1. **Storybook Setup:** 188→ - Installed Storybook 10 with Vite + React + TypeScript 189→ - Configured preview.ts with BlueprintJS styles 190→ - Added to `bun run storybook` script 191→ 192→2. **POCs Created (7 total):** 193→ 194→ | POC | Library | Status | Key Features | 195→ |-----|---------|--------|--------------| 196→ | visx | @visx/brush | ✅ Working | React-native primitives, TypeScript-first | 197→ | Recharts | recharts | ✅ Working | Built-in Brush, easy React integration | 198→ | uPlot | uplot | ✅ Working | Canvas 2D, ~100K pts/ms, Grafana's choice | 199→ | D3 Brush | d3-brush | ✅ Working | Maximum flexibility, imperative API | 200→ | deck.gl Filter | @deck.gl/extensions | ✅ Working | GPU-based filtering, 60K points real-time | Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. file.content: # Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log ## Session: 2025-12-19 (DESIGN_SPEC.md Creation) - IN PROGRESS 🔄 ### Task: Create Implementation Design Specification Document **Objective:** Create `docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md` - an internal technical specification document that will guide the implementation of the demo with Astro.js + deck.gl. Focus on the Planning View with Sandbox Mode for what-if scenarios. **Key Decisions:** - Source of truth: ONLY `docs/RFQ.md` (ignore existing code/docs) - Stack: Astro.js + deck.gl + mocks - Language: English - Primary feature: Planning View with Sandbox Mode (what-if scenarios) - Theme: Dark mode enterprise **RFQ Pain Points to Solve:** 1. No dedicated mileage management tools 2. IBM Maximo only does asset management 3. Manual Excel management 4. Folder-driven data storage 5. Limited maintenance-operations integration 6. Non-compliance risk (underrun/overrun) 7. No predictive simulations 8. Insufficient resource visibility 9. Manual handling delays **Progress:** - ✅ Research: deck.gl, what-if scenario patterns, dashboard design - ✅ Collected visual references (Kepler.gl, Linear, Figma templates) - ✅ Created `docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md` with complete specifications - ✅ Detailed Sandbox Mode (what-if scenarios) as primary feature - ✅ Added moodboard with `` tags for visual references **Document Created: `docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md`** Contents: 1. Visual Mood & Design Philosophy (Linear as reference) 2. Moodboard with `` tags (Kepler.gl, Flowmap, SandDance, Figma templates, Railfleet) 3. Problem → Solution Matrix (all 9 RFQ pain points) 4. Design System (colors, typography, spacing, radius) 5. Screen Specifications (Application Shell, Navbar, Sidebar) 6. Planning View - Semantic Zoom (MACRO/MESO/MICRO) 7. **Sandbox Mode** (PRIMARY FEATURE) - complete specification - Mode Toggle UI - Visual Differentiation (violet accent) - Sandbox Panel with delta metrics - Sandbox Interactions (drag, resize, create, delete) - Compare View (baseline vs scenario) - Saved Scenarios management 8. Components Specification (TaskBlock, KPICard, AlertBanner, Minimap) 9. Data Structures (Train, Task, Scenario entities) 10. Interactions & Gestures 11. Performance Targets 12. Implementation Stack (Astro.js, deck.gl, XState, Tailwind) 13. File Structure (proposed) 14. RFQ Requirements Mapping **Status: COMPLETE ✅** --- ## Session: 2025-12-18 (Semantic Zoom POC) - COMPLETE ✅ ### Task: Complete Semantic Zoom transitions POC with crossfade **Objective:** Test and verify the Semantic Zoom POC that implements smooth crossfade transitions between 4 LOD levels using Hermite smoothstep interpolation. **Completed:** 1. **Semantic Zoom POC Story:** - `apps/canvas/src/stories/01-POCs/Rendering/SemanticZoom.stories.tsx` - 4 LOD levels: Strategic (<0.3x), Tactical (0.3-1.0x), Operational (1.0-3.0x), Execution (>3.0x) - Crossfade transitions with smoothstep Hermite interpolation - Layer opacities panel showing real-time opacity percentages - Interactive zoom slider with threshold markers 2. **LOD Level Visualizations:** - **Strategic (0.15x):** Blue heatmap with percentage cells (50%, 65%, 78%, etc.) - **Tactical (0.60x):** Train IDs list (T-001 to T-016) with quarterly bars label - **Operational (2.00x):** DNA Gantt view with orange styling - **Execution (4.00x):** Detailed task cards with train ID, task type (PM1/PM2/PM3/Overhaul), status badges (completed/in-progress/scheduled) 3. **Crossfade Transitions Verified:** - Strategic→Tactical (at 0.3): Tested at 0.34x - strategic: 30%, tactical: 70% - Tactical→Operational (at 1.0): Tested at 0.94x - tactical: 78%, operational: 22% - Operational→Execution (at 3.0): Tested at 2.94x - operational: 78%, execution: 22% - All transitions use smooth Hermite interpolation (no sudden jumps) 4. **Story Variants Created:** - Default: Interactive slider for testing all transitions - At Strategic Level: Pre-set to 0.15x - At Tactical Level: Pre-set to 0.60x - At Operational Level: Pre-set to 2.00x - At Execution Level: Pre-set to 4.00x **Testing Results:** - All 4 LOD levels render correctly with distinct visuals - Crossfade transitions are smooth and use correct opacity blending - Interactive slider allows seamless navigation between LOD levels - No console errors **Run POC:** ```bash cd apps/canvas && bun run storybook # Navigate to 01-POCs/Rendering/Semantic Zoom ``` **POCs Completed This Session:** - ✅ POC: deck.gl heatmap for Strategic view (previous) - ✅ POC: Gesture recognizer (pan, pinch, momentum) (previous) - ✅ POC: Semantic zoom transitions (crossfade) ← THIS SESSION **Next Tasks:** - Components: GanttBar, KPICard, TodayLine, TimelineHeader - Layer: Strategic (12-year heatmap) - Layer: Tactical (stacked bars) - Layer: Operational (DNA Gantt) - Layer: Execution (detailed Gantt with resources) --- ## Session: 2025-12-18 (XState Viewport Machine POC) - COMPLETE ✅ ### Task: Create XState viewport state machine for semantic zoom **User Request:** Build POCs for the MMS Fractal Canvas demo, starting with XState v5 for state management. User decisions: Use XState (not @mms/toolkit), BlueprintJS 6, ignore dark mode. **Completed:** 1. **Project Structure Setup:** - Created folder structure: `01-POCs/`, `02-Layers/`, `03-Demo/` - Removed default Storybook example pages - Installed XState 5.x and @xstate/react 6.x 2. **Mock Data Configuration:** - `apps/canvas/src/stories/data/mock-config.ts` - Fleet config, LOD thresholds, colors - `apps/canvas/src/stories/data/mock-generator.ts` - Generates 60K+ tasks over 12 years - 128 trains (49 Kinki Sharyo + 79 Alstom), 2024-2036 contract period 3. **XState Viewport Machine:** - `apps/canvas/src/stories/machines/viewport.machine.ts` - States: `idle`, `panning`, `pinching`, `momentum`, `animating` - Events: ZOOM, ZOOM_TO, PAN, PAN_START/MOVE/END, DOUBLE_TAP, RESET, etc. - LOD levels: strategic (<0.3x), tactical (0.3-1.0x), operational (1.0-3.0x), execution (>3.0x) - Smooth animations with easeOutExpo easing - Momentum/inertial panning support 4. **XState Viewport POC Story:** - `apps/canvas/src/stories/01-POCs/State/XStateViewport.stories.tsx` - Interactive canvas with pan/zoom via mouse/touch - Real-time State Inspector showing all context values - Quick Action buttons: Strategic (0.2x), Tactical (0.5x), Operational (1.5x), Execution (4x) - LOD indicator with color-coded thresholds 5. **Bugs Fixed:** - TypeScript type imports (use `import type` for ESM compatibility) - ANIMATION.zoom → ANIMATION.duration.zoom (nested object access) - Animation interpolation using saved start values (not current values) **Testing Results:** - All LOD transitions working correctly - Smooth animations between zoom levels - State machine transitions properly (idle → animating → idle) - Context values update correctly in real-time **Run POC:** ```bash cd apps/canvas && bun run storybook # Navigate to 01-POCs/State/XStateViewport ``` --- ## Session: 2025-12-18 (Storybook Time Scrubber POCs) - COMPLETE ✅ ### Task: Set up Storybook and create POCs for Time Scrubber components **User Request (Portuguese):** "Configure o Storybook no projeto e adicione POCs para todas as melhorias, sugestões, libs, opções listadas no docs/mms-components-research.md" **Completed:** 1. **Storybook Setup:** - Installed Storybook 10 with Vite + React + TypeScript - Configured preview.ts with BlueprintJS styles - Added to `bun run storybook` script 2. **POCs Created (7 total):** | POC | Library | Status | Key Features | |-----|---------|--------|--------------| | visx | @visx/brush | ✅ Working | React-native primitives, TypeScript-first | | Recharts | recharts | ✅ Working | Built-in Brush, easy React integration | | uPlot | uplot | ✅ Working | Canvas 2D, ~100K pts/ms, Grafana's choice | | D3 Brush | d3-brush | ✅ Working | Maximum flexibility, imperative API | | deck.gl Filter | @deck.gl/extensions | ✅ Working | GPU-based filtering, 60K points real-time |

message.content: Os arquivos grandes são os que contem dados historicos, o de progresso é o principal, o de features eu não sei.

content.thinking:
O usuário está dizendo que os arquivos grandes são os que contêm dados históricos. O `claude-progress.txt` (92K) é o principal arquivo de histórico. Ele quer saber sobre o `features.json` também.

Vou verificar o tamanho e conteúdo do features.json para entender melhor, e depois propor uma estratégia de compressão focando principalmente no claude-progress.txt.

content.text: Vou analisar o `features.json` para entender sua estrutura.

content.content:
290 features.json
{
  "project": "Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)",
  "version": "0.5.0",
  "lastUpdated": "2025-12-17",
  "product": {
    "entry": "apps/canvas",
    "description": "Production React app with semantic zoom (Vite + React 18 + deck.gl + BlueprintJS 6)"
  },
  "migration": {
    "status": "COMPLETE_MIGRATION_REQUIRED",
    "from": "React Three Fiber (R3F) + Three.js",
    "to": "deck.gl with OrthographicView (ONLY)",
    "reason": "docs/deck-research/mms-deck-research.md",
    "archive": "docs/archive/r3f-architecture.md",
    "note": "CRITICAL: Current implementation has BOTH R3F and deck.gl. R3F is DEFAULT. Must completely remove R3F and make deck.gl the ONLY rendering system."
  },
  "reference": {
    "entry": "design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html",
    "description": "Standalone POC for visual reference only"
  },
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "DECK-COMPLETE-001",
      "category": "architecture",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Complete R3F Removal - Delete ALL Three.js/R3F code and dependencies",
      "passes": true,
      "notes": "COMPLETE: Removed all R3F/Three.js imports, deleted canvas/, rendering/, spatial/ directories, removed dependencies. App renders with deck.gl only at 120fps.",
      "blockedBy": null,
      "rationale": "Current implementation has BOTH R3F and deck.gl with R3F as default. This defeats the purpose of migration. Must completely remove R3F to achieve the promised benefits: smaller bundle, simpler architecture, better 2D performance.",
      "tasks": [
        "1. Update App.tsx to ONLY use DeckCanvas (remove useDeckGL toggle, remove InfiniteCanvas import)",
        "2. Delete entire apps/canvas/src/canvas/ directory (InfiniteCanvas.tsx + layers/)",
        "3. Delete apps/canvas/src/rendering/ directory (R3F-specific hooks)",
        "4. Remove R3F dependencies: bun remove @react-three/fiber @react-three/drei three @types/three r3f-perf troika-three-utils",
        "5. Update @mms/toolkit state: clean up any R3F-specific state in viewport.ts",
        "6. Remove spatial index R3F integration from apps/canvas/src/spatial/",
        "7. Verify no imports reference deleted files",
        "8. Run typecheck and fix any errors"
      ],
      "successCriteria": [
        "No @react-three/* imports in codebase",
        "No three.js imports in codebase",
        "Bundle size reduced by ~200KB",
        "App renders with deck.gl only",
        "All features work: pan, zoom, LOD transitions, hover, click, sidebar/timeline sync"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "DECK-COMPLETE-002",
      "category": "architecture",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Implement @use-gesture/react for cross-platform input handling",
      "passes": true,
      "notes": "COMPLETE: Created useCanvasGestures hook with pinch/wheel/drag handlers. Integrated into DeckCanvas, disabled deck.gl controller for pan/zoom. Trackpad detection, delta clamping implemented. Visual tests pass - all LOD transitions work, 120fps.",
      "blockedBy": "DECK-COMPLETE-001",
      "rationale": "Research doc (mms-deck-research.md) specifically recommends @use-gesture/react for cross-platform input normalization. It's already installed but NOT used. deck.gl's built-in controller has limitations with Safari trackpad and touch gestures.",
      "research": "docs/deck-research/mms-deck-research.md - Section: 'The recommended solution: @use-gesture/react'",
      "tasks": [
        "1. Create apps/canvas/src/deck/hooks/useCanvasGestures.ts",
        "2. Implement useGesture with onPinch, onWheel, onDrag handlers",
        "3. Add Safari GestureEvent handling (real scale/rotation props)",
        "4. Add trackpad vs mouse detection from event frequency",
        "5. Add delta clamping (MAX_DELTA=50) to prevent wild jumps",
        "6. Integrate with DeckCanvas - replace deck.gl's built-in controller for zoom/pan",
        "7. Add momentum panning (velocity-based) if time permits",
        "8. Test on: Chrome Mac, Safari Mac, Chrome Windows, Firefox"
      ],
      "configuration": {
        "onPinch": "({ offset: [scale], origin: [ox, oy] }) => zoomAtPoint(scale, ox, oy)",
        "onWheel": "({ delta: [dx, dy], ctrlKey }) => ctrlKey handled by onPinch, else pan(-dx, -dy)",
        "onDrag": "({ delta: [dx, dy] }) => pan(dx, dy)",
        "eventOptions": "{ passive: false } // Required for preventDefault"
      },
      "successCriteria": [
        "Trackpad pinch zoom is smooth on Safari Mac",
        "Two-finger scroll pans (not zooms) on all platforms",
        "Mouse wheel zooms toward cursor",
        "Ctrl+wheel zooms (handled by pinch)",
        "No browser zoom triggered (preventDefault working)"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "DECK-COMPLETE-003",
      "category": "performance",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Implement RBush spatial index for viewport culling",
      "passes": true,
      "notes": "COMPLETE: Installed rbush, created RBushSpatialIndex.ts with bulkLoad and getVisibleTasks methods. Integrated into DeckCanvas - spatial index initialized on mount, visibleTasks used for MESO/MICRO layers. All LOD levels verified: MACRO 120fps, MESO 120fps, MICRO 120fps.",
      "blockedBy": "DECK-COMPLETE-001",
      "rationale": "Research doc recommends RBush R-tree for O(log n) spatial queries. Current SimpleSpatialIndex is O(n) placeholder. With 60k tasks, this matters for performance.",
      "tasks": [
        "1. Install RBush: bun add rbush @types/rbush",
        "2. Create apps/canvas/src/deck/spatial/RBushSpatialIndex.ts implementing ISpatialIndex",
        "3. Implement bulk load on startup (tree.load() for all tasks)",
        "4. Implement viewport query with overscan margin",
        "5. Update DeckCanvas to use RBush for visible task calculation",
        "6. Pass visible task IDs to layers instead of full task array",
        "7. Layers filter their data using visible IDs",
        "8. Benchmark: viewport query <5ms for 1000 results"

content.thinking:
Agora entendi melhor:

1. **claude-progress.txt** - 2338 linhas, 92K - Contém histórico de TODAS as sessões. Este é o arquivo que mais precisa de compressão.

2. **features.json** - 290 linhas, 16K - Contém a definição de features do projeto. Este é menor e tem uma estrutura importante. Provavelmente não precisa de tanta compressão.

Estratégia de compressão para o `claude-progress.txt`:

1. **Arquivar sessões antigas** - Mover sessões antigas (antes de uma certa data) para um arquivo de backup
2. **Manter apenas resumo das sessões antigas** - Uma linha por sessão antiga com: data, task, status
3. **Manter detalhes apenas das últimas 3-5 sessões**

Vou propor isso ao usuário e começar a implementar.

content.text:
Entendi! Os números:

| Arquivo | Linhas | Tamanho |
|---------|--------|---------|
| `claude-progress.txt` | 2338 | 92K |
| `features.json` | 290 | 16K |

O `claude-progress.txt` é o problema principal - 92K de histórico. O `features.json` com 290 linhas é razoável (são as features do projeto).

**Estratégia de compressão para `claude-progress.txt`:**

1. **Arquivar sessões antigas** → mover para `claude-progress-archive.txt`
2. **Manter resumo compacto** → 1 linha por sessão antiga
3. **Detalhes completos** → apenas últimas 3-5 sessões

Vou ver quantas sessões existem e a partir de que data podemos arquivar:

content.thinking:
39 sessões no total! Isso é muito. 

Proposta de compressão:
1. **Últimas 5 sessões** → manter completas (detalhes)
2. **Sessões anteriores (34 sessões)** → comprimir para 1-2 linhas cada

Vou propor isso ao usuário e perguntar como quer proceder.

content.text:
**39 sessões** no total! Isso explica os 92K.

**Proposta de compressão:**

| Seção | Sessões | Formato |
|-------|---------|---------|
| **Recentes** | Últimas 5 | Detalhes completos |
| **Histórico** | 34 anteriores | 1 linha: `data - task - status` |

Exemplo do formato comprimido:
```
## Histórico de Sessões (Resumo)
- 2025-12-16: F118 Momentum Panning ✅
- 2025-12-16: F120+F121 Double-click zoom ✅
- 2025-12-16: F122 Fix elastic/jump drag ✅
...
```

Isso reduziria de ~2300 linhas para ~400 linhas (80% menor).

**Quer que eu:**
1. **Faça a compressão agora** - arquivar histórico antigo
2. **Pergunte primeiro sobre quais sessões manter em detalhe**
3. **Mostre preview** do resultado antes de aplicar
